key_insight: MXtalGFlow turns molecular crystal generation from isolated candidate search into sampling a thermodynamically meaningful distribution over crystal structures.
engineering_relevance: This gives crystal structure prediction a principled way to explore polymorph landscapes and prioritize experimentally plausible packings beyond just the lowest-energy hits.
method: Defines a canonical parameterization for molecular crystal structures.
Trains energy-based GFlowNets to match the Boltzmann distribution over crystals.
Analyzes sampled distributions around known polymorphs and additional packing modes.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two molecules under two energy models (Lennard-Jones and Universal Model for Atoms), with analysis of local basins, known polymorphs, and new competitive packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen energy function and space-group parameterization, so distribution quality may be sensitive to modeling assumptions and representation choices.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
